title: grewia multiflora
intro: Grewia multiflora, also known as the Indian plum or phalsa, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Grewia multiflora include treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as having potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Grewia multiflora include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, taken orally as capsules, or applied topically as creams or ointments.
